\nCollaborators: \nConfidential and Internal Use Only\n\nℹ️ Please feel free to add comments on the document.\nIf any questions please contact here \nTable of Contents\nTable of Contents\nAbstract\nResellers Initiatives Overview \u0026 Status\nInitiatives Status\nRoadmap\nGoals of Reseller\u0027s initiatives\nGlossary of terms\nReservations \u0026 Group Bookings (The B2B portal)\nWhy\nUse cases\nHow\nExisting Roles on Feverzone\nYou can find the list of users and roles in the Settings menu\nBusiness Management\nBusiness Types (channels)\nWhen creating a Business manually, you need to select a Business Type\nYou can view the existing businesses under the partner in the \"Settings -\u003e Business\" section:\nBusiness Creation and Registration\nManual Business Registration inside Fever Zone\nCreation of a new Business and its required fields\nRegistering Business manually in several Partners\nBusiness registration autonomously through a website or landing page (Part of the Self Service model)\nReservations Management\nDefining tickets types and prices per channel (or Business Type)\nDefining the max and min tickets allowed by channel\nChanging the limits of min and max tickets that can be purchased per channel can be done inside Feverzone\nReservation Rules\nMaking a Reservation\nRegistered Businesses can make reservations and purchase them through the Fever Zone Business Portal\nOverride Price Cart Module\nConfiguration Options\nAdjustment Distribution\nRounding\nWho can use the adjustment?\nWhen can you use the adjustment?\nThrough the Fever Zone Partner Portal\nReservations Overview\nReservations list view\nPayment Options for Partner and Businesses\nAvailable Payment options per Business and Partner\nPayment Link Module\nCard Payment errors\nThe Delivery Note\nPayment collector\nAvailable Operations on Reservations and Orders\nReschedule Reservations\nModify Reservations (making changes on their tickets)\nAdd or Reduce tickets\nCancel Reservations\nNotes\nSee Reservations Performance\nEmail Notifications\nTransactional Notifications (Reservation Workflow)\nReservation created Email\nReservation purchased Email\nReservation about to expire Email\nReservation expired Email\nReservation cancelled Email\nReservation modified Email\nBusiness users welcome Email\nPassword recovery Email\nPartner Configurable Notifications\nAvailable Notification Types\nBooking Questions\nPre-booking Questions\nPost-booking Questions\nDownloading the reservations booking questions/answers\nLimitations and Particularities of the Business Reservation Tool\nHow to activate the Reservations capabilities\nTime Slot Blocking (TSB)\nExceptions\nCard Exceptions and errors\nAbstract\nThis document explains all the Reseller\u0027s initiatives and capabilities we offer our clients. The document is intended for internal use, to be able to explain in a direct way to all interested people what they need to know about our initiatives. If you don\u0027t find some information in the document and you still have questions, please contact me directly \nResellers Initiatives Overview \u0026 Status\nHere is a table of all the verticals that the Reseller\u0027s team has ownership on:\n\n\u0010\u0012\u001cInitiatives Status\n\u001c\n\u001c\n\u0012\u001c* Initiative\n\u001c* Status\n\u001c* Notes\n\u0012\u001cReservations \u0026 Group Bookings tool\n\u001c\n\u001cNeeds manual activation, working on features to roll it out to all partners.\n\u0012\u001cAmbassadors tool\n\u001c\n\u001cIt has to be configured manually, but we can now offer our partners, specifically those who are festivals, a points accumulation system to later redeem them in our fevezone. The partner will be able to list the prizes and participants will be able to use their particular website to earn points through sales.\n\u0012\u001cAffiliates and RRPP\n\u001c\n\u001cWe are rolling with Kapital. Needs manual creation of RRPP and networks. We are working on the first MVP for Last Tour\n\u0012\u001cPromotional Pages\n\u001c\n\u001cThe pages need to be manually created. It is also not available on iOS. Ask the QA team to create new ones.\n\u0012\u001cB2B Marketplace\n\u001c\n\u001cIn the definition phase\n\u0012\u001cOTAs\n\u001c\n\u001cNot rue,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_st_i":true,"ts_fs_i":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":73,"ei":107,"sm":{"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#ff0000","clr_type":0}}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":109,"ei":199,"sm":{"ts_bgc2_i":false,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":"#00ff00","clr_type":0}}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":219,"ei":2647,"sm":{"ts_un":true,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#1155cc","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":false,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":false,"ts_ff_i":false,"ts_bgc2_i":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_it":false,"ts_va_i":false,"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_sc_i":false,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_bd":false}}],"revision":1}; DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":4000,"s":"For any questions about what is being developed and launched, check the Product Quarters Priorities Document:\n \nGoals of Reseller\u0027s initiatives \nAt Fever, our B2B portal and Affiliate capabilities are built to maximise growth and efficiency in the resales and distribution of experiences and events. Here’s how they benefit our partners:\n\nMarket Expansion: Our channels allow partners to reach broader and more diverse audiences, significantly widening the scope of their offerings.\nOperational Efficiency: We streamline booking and sales processes, reducing administrative burdens and enabling partners to focus on enhancing service quality.\nEnhanced Flexibility: Our tailored tools adapt to specific partner needs, fostering more effective and strategic collaboration.\nRevenue Growth: Partners can boost their sales and generate higher revenues through collaborative marketing strategies and access to new demographics.\nGlossary of terms\nSpeaking the same language is important to fully understand the system and its capabilities. Here are the commonly used terms and definitions:\nPartner: The entity that owns the offer of plans.\nReseller: Sometimes referred to as an entity that sells Partner plans to third parties. However, we usually specify what type of reseller is in these 3 ways:\nBusiness: An entity that resells plans of a partner with their authorisation. They control the end-to-end selling process. They profit from the difference between wholesale and retail.\nOTA (Online Travel Agency): A connection with an agent that allows publishing the partner\u0027s events on the agent\u0027s website. These websites are resellers of the partner\u0027s plans. They earn a commission per sale. For example, GetYourGuide, Tiqets, or Viator.\nAffiliate: An entity that has an arrangement with the partner to sell plans. They earn a commission based on a percentage of sales. For example, influencers, public relations, or it can be a company. There are cases where the partner itself may use this tool for promotions.\nAffiliate Manager: An affiliate is responsible for a group of sub-affiliates, such as a public relations manager.\nSub-Affiliate: An affiliate that belongs to a group managed by an Affiliate Manager. For example, a public relations professional who belongs to a group managed by a manager.\nGroup: A set of multiple people who want to enjoy a specific plan. For example, a group of tourists, students, or company employees.\nReservation / Booking: This is a lock on a series of tickets for a plan that needs to be paid for.\nOrder: A paid Reservation. An order has the QR codes generated from the tickets and is pending for the final client or group to enjoy the event.\nPayment: The way reservations can be paid for.\nCard: The standard payment method with a card.\nBank transfer: A bank transfer to pay for the reservation (not supported right now).\nManual payment: Manual payment is when a reservation is marked as paid manually by the Partner or Business, even though Fever has not received the money.\nCredit / Balance: This method uses a balance instead of money to pay. This means the payment will be discounted from the balance set by the partner. The entity marking the manual payment records this payment as received. This method requires that both Partner and Business reconcile periodically.\nOther: This payment type allows you to manually mark it as paid using other methods. It also allows you to add text for future reference, such as for bank transfers we do not currently have.\nDeposit: It is a payment that is requested in advance in order to reserve tickets.\nCommission: When a reseller makes a sale, there is a commission. This commission is what the partner gives to the reseller to pay for their services.\nEnd-user (Guest): The ultimate person who enjoys the plan purchased.\nReservation Restrictions: Restrictions are rules to ensure that reservations meet certain requirements. For example, there is a restriction on the expiration of a Reservation—it cancels automatically after a certain number of days if it has not been ,"ei":6723,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":6767,"ei":6779,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":6852,"ei":6865,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":7006,"ei":7021,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":7303,"ei":7307,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":7494,"ei":7500,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":7577,"ei":7586,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":7727,"ei":7742,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":7796,"ei":7820,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":7821,"ei":8051,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_bd":true}}],"revision":1}; DOCS_modelChunkLoadStart = new Date().getTime(); DOCS_initialLoadTiming.incrementTime('mp', DOCS_modelChunkLoadStart - DOCS_modelChunkParseStart); DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":8052,"s":"Reservations \u0026 Group Bookings (The B2B portal)\nWhy\nNegotiations with partners increasingly require more functionalities. Specifically, partners want the ability to sell tickets through other agencies or institutions interested in purchasing tickets for groups rather than a single end customer. These groups can include schools, businesses, agencies, and other collectives buying bulk tickets. Partners with static venues often have a large market for bulk ticket sales through agencies and institutions. Our portal aims to manage these business relationships and reservations, channeling them through the Fever Zone platform to centralise operations, increase efficiency and maximise expansion.\nCurrently, partners manage this process internally, manually, or through other platforms. Fever cannot miss the opportunity to channel all this business volume through the Fever Zone platform. This is why we have developed a system capable of managing the entities involved in these group reservations within Fever Zone.\nA classic example is Patrimonio Nacional, where multiple travel agencies sell tickets to groups of tourists, schools, or businesses. \nToday, group reservations or ticket sales for groups typically have a different process than that of a single user through the marketplace. Groups contact the agency(or business) to reserve a date, a budget is negotiated (or not), and the client pays for the reservation. Then the platform sends the tickets to the agency or client. Unlike the marketplace model, there is an intermediate step, which is the reservation. These usually involve higher ticket volumes and different payment methods, such as bank transfers. There is also the possibility of making partial payments for these large reservations.\nThe ultimate goal is for all reservation business from these partners to go through Fever. This system enables partners to manage group reservations efficiently, catering to schools, businesses, and other collectives. By channelling all this business through the Fever Zone platform, we aim to centralise operations, streamline processes, and unlock immense potential for managing and reselling tickets.\nUse cases\nReservations Self-service Model: We offer the option for a partner to completely delegate to Resellers who can independently book and sell their inventory. This is also known as the Patrimonio Nacional (\"National Heritage partner\") model, where Agencies register on a website to then access a private section of Fever Zone to make their own Reservations. Here, a Partner wants other Resellers to resell their tickets to Groups, for example, a Tourist Agency. Having the Business also has full control of the Reservations and can resell their tickets. This use case is typical for providers with many physical venues who don’t want to be directly involved in managing reservations. In this model, there are restrictions on payment methods and reservations to ensure everything flows smoothly. \nReservations Partner mode: A partner may want to sell to groups or through agencies or businesses, but still retain full control. For partners who wish to manage it themselves, they can create user roles that can handle all Reservations inside Feverzone, allowing employees to be registered to manage all the bookings.\nReservations F4B mode: We can even manage reservations as Fever, offering a Reservation management service. Fever for Business does this, acting as if it were the partner managing the reservations and group tickets on behalf of the partner.\nReservations Mixed mode: There is also the possibility of a hybrid model, where businesses register on a website, and then the partner takes care of managing the reservation.\nHow\nWe offer our partners the ability to open their doors to group reservations by activating a feature inside Fever Zone. This covers the “entire” process, from when an agency, business, or group registers as a potential booker with the partner, to ticket validation at the group entrance, including reservation management, payment methods, cancellations, and rescheduling.\n"},{"ty":"ae","et":"list","id":"kix.ip87ng5bnu52","epm":{"le_nb":{"nl_4":{"b_gf":"%4.","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":162.0,"b_gs":"-","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":13,"b_il":180.0},"nl_3":{"b_gf":"%3.","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":126.0,"b_gs":"-","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":10,"b_il":144.0},"nl_2":{"b_gf":"%2.","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":90.0,"b_gs":"-","b_a":2,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":15,"b_il":108.0},"nl_1":{"b_gf":"%1.","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":54.0,"b_gs":"-","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":13,"b_il":72.0},"nl_8":{"b_gf":"%8.","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":f DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":12126,"s":"When the Reservations module is activated, a section will appear in Fever Zone where partners can manage reservations themselves or offer a fully delegated service, allowing businesses to request and pay for reservations autonomously. We provide restrictions and mechanisms to ensure this autonomous service is controlled. The partner will always be able to decide which businesses can make purchases and which cannot, by having the option to block reservation management.\nExisting Roles on Feverzone\nDifferent user roles have different capabilities and permissions with the Reservations feature. You can see and manage the roles in the \"Settings -\u003e Users -\u003e Roles\" section. Here you will also find a capabilities table depending on the Role.\nYou can find the list of users and roles in the Settings menu\n*\nThe Reservation Manager: This role can only manage and create reservations. It is dedicated exclusively to reservations.\nThe Box Office Manager: Besides managing on-site payments, this role may or may not manage reservations. Admis can modify this possibility. If reservations are enabled, this role can create and pay for reservations. Otherwise, they can view and pay for reservations but cannot create new ones.\nAdmin, Event Manager, and Operations Manager: These roles can make reservations like the Reservation Manager.\nAdmins are capable of doing everything, including the most restricted operations on Reservations, they also ignore expiration rules.\nBusiness Management\nOne of the core features of this tool is the management of entities we call Businesses. These entities represent a business or group of people who will have access to their exclusive area on Feverzone to make reservations and purchases from the partner\u0027s inventory. It is important to know the different types that exist, how they can register, modify information, the existing channels, and restrictions.\nBusiness Types (channels)\nBusinesses are classified by their \"Business Type,\" which functions similarly to an internal channel. It\u0027s crucial to understand that an existing channel is already in place for each Business Type, allowing for the assignment of specific ticket types, prices, and behaviors. This categorization is essential because different Business Types, such as schools and travel agencies, operate distinctively. Therefore, when creating a new Business, it must be assigned to one of the predefined Business Types.\nWhen creating a Business manually, you need to select a Business Type\n*\nCurrently, we have the following 12 categories:\nAgency: Tourist agencies or organisations that arrange group visits.\nEducational: Educational organisations such as schools.\nGuide: Organisations that provide group guides.\nCultural: Cultural organisations.\nCorporate: General businesses.\nLarge Group: Organisations that manage large groups.\nPremium Services: Organisations that offer exclusive services to groups.\nSales Representative: For internal sales.\nInternal Operations: Created to categorise businesses established for internal buying and selling operations within a partner network.\nPartnerships: Created to categorise external businesses that are partnerships.\nInternal Comps: Created to categorise businesses that manage complimentary tickets.\nInvitations: Created to categorise entities that receive or manage invitations.\nThere is no difference between Business Types other than the name. They can then be used to customise prices, tickets, and behaviour rules for bookings.\nℹ️ We can’t create more categories. Unless there is a generic category that we are not taking into account. All of these categories are visible and accessible to any partner that wants to create a new Business.\nYou can view the existing businesses under the partner in the \"Settings -\u003e Business\" section:*\nBusiness Creation and Registration\nThe Business entity needs to exist within the Fever Zone to make reservations. Because new reservations require a Business entity. When a Business entity is created, exclusive access to Fever Zone is also granted to that Business. "ts_sc_i":false,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_bd":false}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":15371,"ei":15381,"sm":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":false,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":false,"ts_ff_i":false,"ts_bgc2_i":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_it":false,"ts_va_i":false,"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_sc_i":false,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":15382,"ei":15394,"sm":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":false,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":false,"ts_ff_i":false,"ts_bgc2_i":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_it":false,"ts_va_i":false,"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_sc_i":false,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_bd":false}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":15405,"ei":15450,"sm":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":false,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":false,"ts_ff_i":false,"ts_bgc2_i":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_it":false,"ts_va_i":false,"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_sc_i":false,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_bd":false}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":15604,"ei":15637,"sm":{"ts_bgc2_i":false,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":"#00ff00","clr_type":0}}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":15638,"ei":15711,"sm":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":false,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":"#00ff00","clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":false,"ts_ff_i":false,"ts_bgc2_i":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_it":false,"ts_va_i":false,"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_sc_i":false,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_bd":false}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":15712,"ei":15813,"sm":{"ts_bgc2_i":false,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":"#00ff00","clr_type":0}}},{"ty":"as","st":"text"," DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":16313,"s":"One is to create them directly and manually from Fever Zone.\nThe other is through a web page (landing page manufactured by Fever) that collects all the necessary information and automatically creates the Business under the partner. The web page can be the one F4B created or one created adhoc for a specific partner. Both of them will have a form that will gather the information required to create a new Business.\n\nBoth options will send an email to the registered Business address, requesting the creation of a password to access the Business’s private area. Inside the private area, Businesses will be able to view their reservations, make reservations, and manage them (if they have permission to do so). \n\nA partner can always decide if the Business has the possibility to make new Reservations or not. But any Business is able to pay them. If a Partner does not want them to make reservations, they simply need to go to the Business list and remove the permissions to create and modify reservations.\n\nThe private business area allows the business to manage all aspects of the reservations, except the possibility of paying them on-site.\nManual Business Registration inside Fever Zone \nA Business can be manually created from Fever Zone. Partners can register a Business through Fever Zone under “Reservations” \u003e “Businesses” \u003e “Add New Business.” https://partners.feverup.com/reservations/businesses/new\n\nCreation of a new Business and its required fields\n*\n\nSeveral fields are required to fill out, including the company’s name, address, email, contact person, TAX number, etc. This information is used to identify the Reseller or Customer who is making the Reservation.\n\nThe card payment for the reservations is enabled by default for all businesses. To enable payment using a credit balance, you must use this screen to configure it along the credit currency and amount.\n\nIn addition to payment details, payment instructions can also be added, which will be reflected in the Delivery Note for each reservation.\n\nPartners and Business can configure their B2B email communications language preferences. After setting a new language with the communications language selector, new emails for the selected business will be sent in the desired language. There may be some cases in which the email template is not translated for the selected language, in which case it will default to English for communications. (check email communications below for more info on the emails sent).\n\nChanging the Business communications language\u000b*\n\nAs a partner you can edit certain fields of the Business (https://partners.feverup.com/reservations/businesses/{business_id}) . \n\nBut only the Business, by accessing their own portal can modify all of their details on Setting -\u003e Business Info (https://partners.feverup.com/settings/profile)\n\nAvailable language options are: \u000b\nEnglish\nGerman\nSpanish\nFrench\nItalian\nKorean\nPortuguese\n\u000bThis feature doesn’t affect the direct communications with clients, which are configured from the cart.\nRegistering Business manually in several Partners\nℹ️ A business can be registered with multiple partners (the same email should be used when registering businesses for multiple partners).\nHowever, this email can’t be used by any other kind of user (for example Reservation Manager). And the same email can’t be used to create multiple businesses for the same partner.\n\n⚠️ When registering a business with multiple partners, the same business type must be selected. A business is restricted to having only one business type.\nBusiness registration autonomously through a website or landing page (Part of the Self Service model)\nIn addition to manually registering businesses in Fever Zone, we can automate the process by linking a website\u0027s landing page to collect necessary data and automatically create businesses in FZ for partners. The new business will then appear in the business list. This method is particularly useful for partners who prefer not to manage this process DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":20322,"s":"\nIt is important to note that for security reasons, businesses created using this automated method can only process card payments. All relevant restrictions defined for each business type will still apply.\n\nFor further details on integrating the Business Registration API with a landing page, please refer to the Business Registration API tab.\nReservations Management\nSeveral options exist for modifying reservation behavior, limits, and rules.\nDefining tickets types and prices per channel (or Business Type)\nIt is possible to define the types of tickets and their prices that will be displayed in the Reservation process. This functionality is especially useful for establishing differentiated rates, for example, for educational institutions or tourism agencies.\nTo manage tickets and their prices, it is essential to have the Channels view activated in Feverzone. Within this section, you must search for the channel corresponding to the type of client you want to configure. For example, for the Agencies Type, there is a channel called Resellers_Agency.\nOnce the channel is selected, you will be able to choose from the existing ticket types the ones you want to allow to be purchased by that channel. \nℹ️ By default, channels do not have tickets assigned. You won’t be able to make a Reservation because no tickets will be shown. Therefore, after activating the Bookings function, it is crucial to publish the desired tickets in the corresponding channels.\nConsider an example: There are three types of tickets (Adult, Child, and Teacher), with the Teacher ticket being free and limited to two per booking. The configuration could be as follows:\nFor businesses categorized as \"Educational\", the Teacher ticket will be displayed and therefore allowed to be “purchased”.\nFor non-\"Educational\" businesses, such as \"Corporate\", this ticket will not be displayed.\nIn this way, a school registered in the \"Educational\" channel will be able to select the Teacher tickets when accessing Feverzone and make a Reservation.\n\nSame happens with prices. The price list functionality (Events -\u003e Inventory Setup -\u003e Pricing) allows to set specific prices per channel. Therefore stablishing multiples strategies to different types of Businesses Types.\nDefining the max and min tickets allowed by channel\nWhen creating a reservation, you select the number of tickets for a specific type (for example: 3 tickets for Adults). Sometimes you need to have different restrictions per channel (such as Marketplace and Reservations), and this happens because the reservation groups are sometimes 20, 30…100 people. To achieve this, you must go to the ticket type in Feverzone and in the ticket types tab you can edit the limit for the reservation channel you want.\nChanging the limits of min and max tickets that can be purchased per channel can be done inside Feverzone\n*\n⚠️ Modifying tickets allowed may interfere with Business Reservation Rules (Check Business Reservation Rules). The most restrictive rule will always apply when creating reservations.\n\nExample: if configuring 1 and 50 as min and max tickets for one specific ticket type will allow businesses to purchase between 1 and 50. \nIf we also configure the minimum number of tickets per Reservation to 8, businesses will need to purchase between 8 and 50.\nReservation Rules \nReservations must have a defined period for their completion and an expiration date. If Reservations do not expire, they will block inventory and tickets without corresponding payments being received. To avoid this situation, there are rules that streamline booking management for Business. Check Tab Business Reservation Rules\nMaking a Reservation\nThere are two ways to make a reservation:\nThrough the Fever Zone Business Portal (As a Business)\nThrough the Fever Zone Partner Portal (As a Partner)\nRegistered Businesses can make reservations and purchase them through the Fever Zone Business Portal\nThis is one of the main advantages of the Reservations module: allowing businesses to manage their own bookings. Allowing partners to forget about any management, with all the information, data and control on what each business is doing centralised in DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":24503,"s":"\u000bTo make a reservation from the Business portal, the Business must have its account created and set up its password. It must also have sessions published on their channel. Then, it can make reservations by accessing its portal. To make a reservation, they must go to the Reservations section -\u003e Make a Reservation -\u003e Select a plan -\u003e Add to cart  -\u003e Tickets receiver data -\u003e Make reservation / Pay Now.\nBusiness payment options\n\nThe reservation can be paid for immediately using the payment method enabled for that Business. Once a reservation is paid, it becomes an order and can be viewed in both the Reservations section.\nDifferent ticket selectors\nWhen you enter the ticket selection page you might see different layouts for the different types of events. You can configure this on the backoffice in the section “session selector type”*\n\nTIMELESS\nIf the event is timeless, you will neither see the date selector (calendar), nor the date \u0026 hour selectors: \n*\nBY DATE\nIf the event is by date, you will see the date selector (calendar) and the date (individual dates) but not the hour selector: \n\n*\n\nBY DATE AND HOUR\nIf the event is by date and hour , you will see the date selector (calendar), the date selector (individual dates) and the time selector:*\n: \nOverride Price Cart Module\nThe Override module allows you to apply variations to the final price of a reservation, either by increasing or decreasing its value. This functionality is useful when adjustments to the booking price are required due to discounts, additional costs, or manual corrections. The module shows right after you select the tickets you want to add to the Reservation on the Contact Details screen.\n\nConfiguration Options\nYou can configure the Override using the following parameters:\nOperation: Determines whether the adjustment adds to or subtracts from the final price.\nCalculation: Defines how the adjustment is calculated. It can be either fixed or percentage-based:\u2028  \nFixed: A specific value is added or subtracted from the total amount.\u2028  \nPercentage: A percentage of the total amount is calculated and applied as an increase or decrease.\n\nAdjustment Distribution\nThe adjustment is evenly distributed across all tickets in the reservation, either increasing or decreasing each ticket’s price proportionally. For instance, suppose you have a reservation with two tickets priced at €10 each, and two different tickets priced at €5 each. If you add €20 to the final price, the first two tickets will now cost €20 each, and the other two €15 each. The same logic applies when reducing the total price.\nNote: This price distribution also applies to management fees, but only to variable ones.\n\nRounding\nSince the discount or increase is distributed among the ticket prices — which are rounded to two decimal places — some fractional values may be lost during the rounding process.\nThese lost decimal amounts are displayed in the Rounding field, representing the residual difference caused by rounding adjustments across all tickets.\n\nWho can use the adjustment? \nThe adjustment only can be used by the role admin but you can view the applied adjustment in the reservation detail and order detail\n\nWhen can you use the adjustment?\nThe adjustment can be used in three scenarios\nWhen you are making a reservation\nWhen you increase the reservation items (only if the reservation has adjustment)\nWhen you reduce the reservation items (only if the reservation has adjustment)\nIf a reservation has override, when you increase or decrease the reservation items only can modify the adjustment value, you can’t disable the adjustment\n\nOverride module\n*\nExample of reservation detail with override\n*\nExample of order detail with override*\nThrough the Fever Zone Partner Portal\nMaking a reservation through a Partner user requires selecting the Business and then making the reservation. When done through a Partner user, the restrictions that apply to the Business do not apply. This reservation method is called a Reservation on Behalf since each Reservation requires a Business and the Partner does it on behalf of another _color":"#666666","clr_type":0},"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":27955,"ei":28107,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#666666","clr_type":0},"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":28108,"ei":28108,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_it_i":false}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":28109,"ei":28109,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_it_i":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":28110,"ei":28125,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_it_i":false}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":28126,"ei":28126,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#666666","clr_type":0},"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":28128,"ei":28171,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_it_i":false}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":28174,"ei":28212,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_it_i":false}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":28488,"ei":28508,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}}],"revision":1}; DOCS_modelChunkLoadStart = new Date().getTime(); DOCS_initialLoadTiming.incrementTime('mp', DOCS_modelChunkLoadStart - DOCS_modelChunkParseStart); DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":28608,"s":"\n\u0010\u0012\u001cℹ️ As the restrictions don’t apply for this case, the partner can create a reservation even 1 minute before the event. If so, the expiration works as follow:\nIf the reservation expiration can be configured following the expiration policy in place, it\u0027s done that way.\nIf not, for example when creating reservations for the last minute, the reservation expires at the end of the day of the event.\nReminder: When the business tries to create the reservation, they can only create them if there is room for the expiration policy. If they try to create a reservation 1 day before, they must pay it immediately.\n\u0011\n\u0010\u0012\u001cℹ️ Some Partners who manage reservations choose to create a Business with the Partner’s credentials and make all Reservations on Behalf through it in order to receive all communications.\n\u0011\nReservations Overview\nThere\u0027s a view where you can see all the reservations made that are pending payment and those that have been paid. In this view, you have many options, such as filtering reservations to find them more easily, downloading a CSV with the filter results, and even managing these reservations.\n\nThis list can be filtered by date, reservation status, and identifier, and searched by Business name or email.\n\nReservations list view\n*\nPayment Options for Partner and Businesses\nHere are the available payment methods:\nPayment Link: After a reservation is made, a link can be generated that directs the user to a checkout page to pay the remaining amount. Anyone with the link can complete the payment if logged in. Upon successful payment, the reservation converts into an order, and the payer\u0027s details are recorded in the Order Details screen. This method enables secure payments without requiring card details to be shared.\nDirect Pay: Payments can be processed directly within the Feverzone by inputting a valid bank card\u0027s PAN, expiration date, and CVV. This is particularly useful for sales conducted over the phone. Entering the card details immediately pays for the reservation and transforms it into an order.\nCredit / Balance: Partners can assign a balance to Businesses, which is configured on the Business details screen. This balance can be set to any amount and currency, allowing the Business to pay for reservations without direct financial transactions. The conditions for setting a balance are at the Partner\u0027s discretion. For example, a Partner might request an upfront payment from a Business, then adjust the balance on the Business profile. This enables the Business to make multiple purchases without immediate payment. When the balance is depleted, the Business will no longer be able to pay using this method. Partners can also agree to an initial balance and later reconcile based on tickets sold. This facilitates high-volume businesses to make purchases and settle accounts with the Partner, for instance, at the end of the month. Businesses can only view their remaining balance on the checkout screen when paying for a reservation.\nMark as Manually Paid: This option is used when a Partner chooses not to collect payment via Fever\u0027s available methods (e.g., payment link) but still needs to issue tickets. Marking a reservation as paid confirms that the Partner has collected the full payment. When this option is selected, the system requires the Partner to indicate the manual payment method used for traceability. A note can be added to the order for tracking purposes, such as a bank transfer reference if the customer paid the Partner directly.\nPay at the Box Office: This option is for reservations that require payment at the ticket sales location or venue. It immediately activates a payment option at the Box Office, allowing the reservation to be paid using configured terminals or cash at the register. If a Fever Box Office terminal is used, Fever collects the money. If cash is used, the Partner collects the money.\nAvailable Payment options per Business and Partner\nPartners have access to a full range of payment options, while Businesses are limited to credit/balance, card payments (or payment Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":32677,"s":"\n\u0010\u0012\u001c\n\u001cPartner\n\u001cBusiness\n\u0012\u001cPayment Link\n\u001cyes\n\u001cyes\n\u0012\u001cDirect Pay\n\u001cyes\n\u001cyes\n\u0012\u001cCredit / Balance\n\u001cyes\n\u001cyes\n\u0012\u001cMark as paid\n\u001cyes\n\u001cno\n\u0012\u001cPay at the box office\n\u001cyes\n\u001cno\n\u0011\n*\nPayment Link Module\nThe Payment Link module offers a quick way to create a link that once opened it redirects the user to the checkout page of that Reservation. Anyone with this link, and who is logged in, can access the checkout page and finalize the payment.\nThe Payment Link Module offers two options:\nCopy the link, to be pasted and shared anywhere.\nSend the link via email to the address stored in the reservation’s contact information. This will send an email to the Contact email, set on the previous step. If you are managing a Whitelabel, please contact Resellers to edit the custom email template that will hold the link for that partner.\n\nAvailable configuration options of the link before sending it:\nApplication Selection\nDefines the base URL, branding, and UI style of the checkout page. This is helpful for whitelabels since the URL will be the one of the Whitelabel and not fever. If this module is active on a whitelabel event, and you don’t see multiple options, please contact the Resellers team.\nLanguage Selection\nDetermines the language used in the checkout page and the email content.\nAvailable languages are restricted to:\nThe languages supported by the reservation’s plan.\nDefault language:\nThe language of the Reseller/Business.\nWhen a different language is selected:\nThe cart language is updated to the chosen language.\nThat language becomes the default language for the reservation going forward.\n\n*\n\nCard Payment errors\n\nThe Delivery Note\nThe Delivery Note is a very useful document that serves as a packing slip or purchase inventory. It is not an invoice and cannot be used as such. The delivery note contains various details such as the name of the business, the name of the partner, purchase information, the items and quantities purchased, payment instructions, and a space for a signature.\n\nThe Delivery Note can be downloaded at any time from the reservation details screen by the Partner and Business.\n\nOnce the reservation is paid and the order is generated, the delivery note adds a QR code that can be validated at the venue using our validation app, which contains all the tickets for the Order. This is useful for very large Orders with many tickets to avoid having to print them all.\n\nThe utility of the delivery note is to provide information, for example, to serve as a quote document to send to the client prior to payment.\n\nThe DN can include details on how to pay for these items, such as the bank account for payment or other types of instructions. These instructions are configured individually for each Business. This description can be modified on the details screen of each Business.\n\nPayment collector\nKnowing the Payment Collector is crucial when paying for Reservations. It indicates who received the money per each finished transaction, and it is vital to have it well defined on each purchase.\n\nThere are two options: fever or partner. Fever means our company is the one who gathered the money and partner means it was the partner who has the funds.\n\nFor reconciliation, fees and paying partners it’s vital to know who gathered the money. There is a table that stores the payment collector configurations. The money recollected by each purchase can be looked up in the payment collector attribute in the order table.\n\nThe Payment Collector can vary depending on the payment method. For card payments, Fever always collects the money, but Partners have other options that need to be configured, like manually marking payments as paid. Also F4B has the possibility to decide who was the collector since sometimes they help the partner in their Reservation operations.\n\nFor further details, please refer to the Payment Collector Wiki documentation \nAvailable Operations on Reservations and Orders\nWhat you can do with a Reservation or an Order depends on the role who is using the tool and its status. Depending if it is a Partner or a Business, paid or unpaid different actions will be available.\n"},{"ty":"ae","et":"inline","id":"kix.ev6aa6wwgwil","epm":{"ee_eo":{"eo_bo":{"ln_s":0,"ln_w":0.0,"ln_c2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0}},"i_pid":null,"eo_mb":9.0,"i_ht":381.6677490234397,"eo_rtdf":{"rdf_ft":0},"eo_ml":9.0,"eo_mr":9.0,"eo_ad":null,"i_cid":"s-blob-v1-IMAGE-37lNmW7FoeE","i_cont":0.0,"eo_mt":9.0,"eo_type":0,"eo_lco":{"lc_oi":"","lc_cs":"","lc_ct":0,"lc_sci":"","lc_srk":""},"i_wth":816.01969653171,"eo_at":null,"i_iwc":{"iwc_w":false},"eo_hb":false,"eo_rtd":"","i_crop":{"crop_oxr":0.0,"crop_oyr":0.0,"crop_rot":0.0,"crop_hr":1.0,"crop_wr":1.0},"i_iw":false,"i_rot":0.0,"i_clst":{"cv":{"op":"set","opValue":[]}},"i_bri":0.0,"i_msct":0,"i_src":"","i_opa":1.0}}},{"ty":"ae","et":"inline","id":"kix.ml85d59g6y5","epm":{"ee_eo":{"eo_bo":{"ln_s":0,"ln_w":0.0,"ln_c2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0}},"i_pid":null,"eo_mb":9.0,"i_ht":286.7484596385784,"eo_rtdf":{"rdf_ft":0},"eo_ml":9.0,"eo_mr":9.0,"eo_ad":null,"i_cid":"s-blob-v1-IMAGE-ES1MQuGBy1g","i_cont":0.0,"eo_mt":9.0,"eo_type":0,"eo_lco":{"lc_oi":"","lc_cs":"","lc_ct":0,"lc_sci":"","lc_srk":""},"i_wth":512.4440186211245,"eo_at":null,"i_iwc":{"iwc_w":false},"eo_hb":false,"eo_rtd":"","i_crop":{"crop_oxr":0.0,"crop_oyr":0.0,"crop_rot":0.0,"crop_hr":1.0,"crop_wr":1.0},"i_iw":false,"i_rot":0.0,"i_clst":{"cv":{"op":"set","opValue":[]}},"i_bri":0.0,"i_msct":0,"i_src":"","i_opa":1.0}}},{"ty":"ae","et":"list","id":"kix.nm55ewibhvlg","epm":{"le_nb":{"nl_4":{"b_gf":"%4.","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":162.0,"b_gs":"","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":13,"b_il":180.0},"nl_3":{"b_gf":"%3.","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":36793,"s":"\nAvailable operations for Reservations (unpaid ones)\nThe Partner is able to do everything\nPay the Reservation with all payment methods available\nVia payment link \nDirectly in Fever Zone with a card\nMark it as Paid manually\nPay in Box Office \nPay with the Business Balance\nView/Download the Delivery Note\nIncrease or decrease tickets\nCancel the reservation\nReschedule the reservation\nAdd or modify note\nThe Business has limited options\nPay the Reservation\nWith credit card or balance (depending on the Business setting)\nPay via payment link \nReschedule the Reservation\nCancel\nIncrease or reduce tickets\nAdd or modify a note\nView/Download the delivery note\n\nAvailable operations for Orders (Paid Reservations)\nThe Partner will be able to do everything except the payment as it was already done.\nCancel fully or partially the Order if supported.\nReschedule (with limitations)\nModify the tickets (with limitations)\nPrint Tickets\nConfirm the arrival of tickets at the venue\nConfirm that tickets have been validated\nThe Business will be able to:\nView the Order details\nView/Download the Delivery Note\nPrint tickets (for printing on a ticket printer)\n\nReschedule Reservations\nYou can Reschedule Reservations and Orders except for the ones with seating.\nModify Reservations (making changes on their tickets)\nYou can modify Reservations (allows changes only for existing tickets). An Order coming from Resellers can’t modify their tickets.\nAdd or Reduce tickets\nYou can add or reduce tickets on the Reservation but only the ones that already exist. The Orders can’t change their tickets coming from a Reservation.\nCancel Reservations\nAllows to cancel a Reservation. An email will be sent to the ticket receiver. Depending on the payment method the money will be returned to the card that paid it.\nNotes\nYou can add or edit notes to keep track of important information. There are three types of notes:\nReservation Note\nCan be added, edited, or deleted when a reservation is created but not yet paid.\nUseful for recording details before payment.\nAlso visible in the validation app.\nCan be managed by both business users and resellers.\u000b\nOrder Note\nCreated automatically or manually once the reservation is paid.\nAny existing Reservation Note is transferred here.\nKeeps track of information about the completed order.\nAlso visible in the validation app.\nCan be managed by both business users and resellers.\u000b\nPayment Note\nAdded when a payment is made via manual payment..\nRecords payment-specific information.\nNot visible in the validation app.\nCannot be deleted by the user.\n\nSee Reservations Performance\nPartners can see how their sales are going. There\u0027s a dashboard set up to view all your reservations by plan. To access it, go to Dashboard -\u003e Event Sales -\u003e Reservations.\nEmail Notifications\nThere are two types of email notification systems:\nTransactional Notifications - Automated emails sent during the reservation workflow (creation, payment, expiration, etc.). Notifications sent to the Business can be changed to a different language in the Business profile.\nPartner Configurable Notifications - Partner-level notifications that can be enabled/disabled from Feverzone Settings. The language in which these emails are sent is defined by the language in which the Businesses/Partners were created. There is no current way to select select modify the language of these emails.\nTransactional Notifications (Reservation Workflow)\nIn the Reservations flow, various email notifications are sent under different circumstances. Here you can see a list of the purpose and when it is sent.\nReservation created Email\nPurpose: Inform the business that the reservation has been created and give information about the date, venue, price, etc. and give a link to pay it.\nWhen is it sent?: Once the reservation is created.\nReservation purchased Email\nPurpose: Inform the business that the reservation has been purchased and paid for and give information about the date, venue, price, etc.\nWhen is it sent? Once the reservation is paid for or directly purchased. This is sent to the contact info.\n"},{"ty":"ae","et":"list","id":"kix.33dmcs5jo4ay","epm":{"le_nb":{"nl_4":{"b_gf":"%4","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":162.0,"b_gs":"○","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":9,"b_il":180.0},"nl_3":{"b_gf":"%3","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":126.0,"b_gs":"●","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":9,"b_il":144.0},"nl_2":{"b_gf":"%2","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":90.0,"b_gs":"■","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":9,"b_il":108.0},"nl_1":{"b_gf":"%1","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":54.0,"b_gs":"○","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":9,"b_il":72.0},"nl_8":{"b_gf":"%8","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":fals ":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":40405,"ei":40545,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":40546,"ei":40563,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_it_i":false}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":40564,"ei":40596,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":40625,"ei":40633,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":40634,"ei":40762,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":40763,"ei":40779,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":40780,"ei":40869,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}}],"revision":1}; DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":40870,"s":"Reservation about to expire Email\nPurpose: Inform the business that the reservation is about to expire and give a link to pay it.\nWhen is it sent?: It’s sent twice. 48 hours and 24 hours before the reservation expires (if the reservation has not been paid before)\nReservation expired Email\nPurpose: Inform the business that the reservation has expired.\nWhen is it sent?: Once the reservation has expired.\t\nReservation cancelled Email\nPurpose: Inform the business that the reservation has expired.\nWhen is it sent?: Once the reservation is cancelled.\nReservation modified Email\nPurpose: To inform the business that the reservation has been modified, provide information about the new date and a link to pay for it.\nWhen is it sent?: Once the reservation is modified.\nBusiness users welcome Email\nPurpose: To inform the business that a new account has been created and provide a link to set the password and log in to FeverZone.\nWhen is it sent?: Once the business is created.\nPassword recovery Email\nPurpose: Give the business the possibility to recover their password.\nWhen is it sent?: Once the business tries to recover their password.\n\nPartner Configurable Notifications\nThese notifications are partner-level alerts that can be enabled or disabled from Settings \u003e Notifications in Feverzone. They keep partners informed about critical business events.\n\n*\n\nAvailable Notification Types\nOrder Purchase Notification\nPurpose: Alert partner when an order is purchased\n\n*\nSession Expired Notification\nPurpose: Notify partner when a session expires with attendee list\n\t\n*\n\nPlan Sold Out Notification\nPurpose: Alert partner when an event reaches full capacity\n\t*\n\nReservation Created Notification\nPurpose: Notify partner when a business creates a new reservation (not paid)\n\t\n\t*\n\nTicket Cancellation Notification - Staff only\nPurpose: Alert partner when tickets are cancelled (only for the events occurring within the next 7 days)\n*\n\nTicket Session Changed Notification - Staff only\nPurpose: Notify partner when ticket session details change\n*\n\nBooking Questions\nWhen handling reservations, there are multiple workflows where booking questions can be asked if they are configured in the plan reserved.\nPre-booking Questions\nThese questions are those that are asked before payment, can be both optional or not. If required the reservation workflow can not finish unless answered.\nMaking a reservation\nWhen making a reservation they should be displayed just before the reservation summary screen\nIncreasing/Reducing number of tickets of a reservation\nWhen updating a reservation the pre-booking questions should appear just after making the modification\nRescheduling a reservation\nAgain, as it counts as a reservation modification the pre-booking questions appear after making the change\nPost-booking Questions\nThese are the questions made after the purchase, they can’t be mandatory, the user can cancel the answer and the workflow will not be interrupted.\nMark manually as paid\nWhen marking a reservation as paid manually a modal should appear just before the payment summary screen.\nDownloading the reservations booking questions/answers\nThe booking questions and answers are automatically added to the pdf downloaded when clicking the Download button in the reservation listing screen.\n\n*\n\n\nLimitations and Particularities of the Business Reservation Tool\nWe do not yet support bookings that are half-paid or partially paid or that have received a deposit. They must be paid in full.\nThanks to the fact that the Businesses are grouped by type (each type is a channel), different plans and prices can be assigned to different groups. For example, schools can have a different ticket/plan/price type than corporate groups.\nReservations that haven’t been paid can be completed in the Box Office. They just need to be opened to pay by a Box Office Role or click on “Pay in Box Office”\nRight now, we don’t automatically manage bank transfers; however, you can use the Delivery Note to send payment details, Notes within Orders, and the Mark Reservation as Paid functionality to assist with true,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":41878,"ei":41938,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":41939,"ei":41956,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":41957,"ei":42008,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":42009,"ei":42043,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#666666","clr_type":0},"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":42044,"ei":42907,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#666666","clr_type":0},"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":42909,"ei":42909,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43244,"ei":43264,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43265,"ei":43358,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43359,"ei":43413,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43414,"ei":43516,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43517,"ei":43543,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43544,"ei":43727,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43728,"ei":43733,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43734,"ei":43820,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43821,"ei":43842,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":43843,"ei":44101,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":44102,"ei":44109,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":44110,"ei":44152,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":44153,"ei":44154,"sm":{"ts_it":true,"ts_it_i":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":44155,"ei":44155,"sm":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_bd":true}},{"ty":"as","st":"text","si":44156,"e DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"is","ibi":44958,"s":"A Business can be registered under multiple Partners, but the Business can’t be added more than once to the same Partner. The email can’t be in use by other kinds of users (see Business Registration).\nWe support the rescheduling of reservations.\nThere is no way to delete a Business. The B2B business team must be requested to delete it from the database. Ask the Business team to delete it.\nA Partner cannot validate a Business, but they can remove reservation permissions if needed. They can also be created without reservation permission, requiring the partner to activate it manually. There is currently no planned solution for a Business validation system.\nFor invoicing features contact \nConfiguring commissions for businesses within the fever zone is not possible. \nPartial payments for a reservation cannot be made; the full amount must be paid.\nHow to activate the Reservations capabilities\nℹ️ Ask for the Tool through the JIRA ticketing tool here:\n https://feverup.atlassian.net/servicedesk/customer/portal/2/group/95/create/396 \nTime Slot Blocking (TSB)\nThe Time Slot Blocking/Exclusivity system guarantees the exclusivity of the booked experience. Designed for activities such as escape room sessions and museum visits, the system ensures that once a reservation or order is made, the entire time slot is exclusively reserved for that group, regardless of who books it. \nThis means that the complete experience at that specific hour is blocked, preventing any other user from making a reservation for the same time slot. Additionally, the system automatically updates the status of all associated sessions to \"sold out\", ensuring a consistent and seamless booking experience. \n\nCheck the definition for TSB on the Time Slot Blocking tab\n\nExceptions\nCard Exceptions and errors\nHere is a list of error with their explanation of the card payment process\n\n\u0010\u0012\u001cStripe error\n\u001cFeverZone Message\n\u0012\u001cauthentication_required\n\u001cYour bank requires additional verification to complete this payment. Please confirm authentication and try again.\n\u0012\u001capprove_with_id\n\u001cYour bank has declined the payment. Please try again or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001ccall_issuer\n\u001cYour bank has declined the payment. Please try again or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001ccard_not_supported\n\u001cYour card can’t be used for this operation. Please try another payment method or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001ccard_velocity_exceeded\n\u001cYour bank didn’t authorize the payment, possibly due to insufficient funds or a spending limit.\n\u0012\u001ccurrency_not_supported\n\u001cYour card can’t be used for this operation. Please try another payment method or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001cdo_not_honor\n\u001cYour bank has declined the payment. Please try again or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001cdo_not_try_again\n\u001cYour bank has declined the payment. Please try again or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001cduplicate_transaction\n\u001cYour bank has declined the payment. Please try again or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001cexpired_card\n\u001cThis card can no longer be used. Please add a new one or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001cfraudulent\n\u001cWe couldn’t complete your payment. Please use another payment method or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001cgeneric_decline\n\u001cYour bank has declined the payment. Please try again or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001cincorrect_cvc\n\u001cWe couldn’t validate your card details. Please check them and try again.\n\u0012\u001cincorrect_number\n\u001cWe couldn’t validate your card details. Please check them and try again.\n\u0012\u001cincorrect_pin\n\u001cThis card can no longer be used. Please add a new one or contact your bank.\n\u0012\u001cincorrect_zip\n\u001cWe couldn’t validate your card details. Please check them and try again.\n\u0012\u001cinsufficient_funds\n\u001cYour bank didn’t authorize the payment, possibly due to insufficient funds or a spending limit.\n\u0012\u001cinvalid_account\n\u001cThis card can no longer be used. Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.9pm8deja97ke"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"Reservations with Seating\nLatest version: \nReservations with Seating enable Partners to reserve seats manually instead of automatically assigning them. Details, limitations, activation, plan setup, and testing guidelines are included.\nTable of Contents\nTable of Contents\nWhy\nUse cases\nHow\nUser Manual\nLimitations\nHow to activate\n\nWhy\nThis feature enables partners to make reservations for Events that have seating configurations, allowing them to select specific seats manually rather than relying on automatic seat assignment. When a Reservation is made, these reserved seats disappear from the public until the Reservation expires or is cancelled.\n\nℹ️ Internal presentation for the BDE (dated 08/05/2025): \nUse cases\nMaking New Reservations: Partners can create Reservations for Events with seating configurations using the standard reservation process, with one key addition: a free seating selector map allows Partners to choose specific seats. Once seats are selected, the Reservation details will include all seating information.\nRescheduling Existing Reservations: When rescheduling a Reservation with seating, partners select a new date and time, then use the seating selector map to choose available seats for the new session. Since seat availability may differ between sessions, the system allows session type changes and automatically recalculates the final price during the summary step.\nHow\nWe\u0027ve introduced a new seat state called Reserved to our existing seat management system. Previously, seats could only be:\nFree - Available for booking\nHeld - Temporarily held during the booking process\nBooked - Purchased and confirmed.\n\nThe new Reserved state makes seats unavailable to the public (similar to booked seats) while remaining unpaid, giving partners control over seat allocation before final purchase.\nUser Manual\nA test plan is available in Fever Zone under partner \"SO Test\" for testing purposes:\nhttps://partners.feverup.com/plans/dashboard/116639\nMake a reservation\nThe seat reservation process follows the existing Reservation workflow. The seating selector map will appear automatically when applicable, after the selection of the day, prompting an intuitive seat selection.\n*\nReschedule a reservation\nWhen rescheduling a reservation with seating, the system loads the seating map for your selected new date and time. You must choose the same number of seats as your original reservation to proceed. Since different session types are supported, the system will automatically recalculate the final price at the end of the rescheduling process to reflect any changes.\n*\nResults\nWhen the purchase is completed, the seat status changes from \"reserved\" to \"booked\" and appears in the order details.\n*\nLimitations\nPlan Configuration Dependency: This tool is designed specifically for free seating map selector configurations. This means the functionality is designed for the specific configuration where all ticket types can be selected simultaneously, in other words, a multi-ticket cart. Plans not properly configured may cause unexpected behaviour.\nReservation Modifications Not Supported: Increasing or decreasing the number of seats in existing reservations is not currently supported. These features are planned for future releases. If you need additional seats, create a new separate Reservation.\nHow to activate\nThe functionality is controlled by the reservations_with_seating feature flag. When deactivated, the reservation process will prevent users from continuing with seat-based reservations.\n\nPlans must be configured to use free seating map selector mode. Set the Session selector type to \"By free selection map\" in the plan DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.gvatx216q5zl"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"Seating Channeling\nLatest version: \nSeating Channelling enables Partners to exclusively allocate seats to specific selling channels, like for invitations, per Fever Zone timeslot. It requires a Feature Flag, depends on main plan publishing, and prohibits dual-channel assignments.\nTable of Contents\nTable of Contents\nWhy\nUse cases\nHow\nUser Manual\nLimitations\nHow to activate\n\nWhy\nA feature that allows partners to assign seats to selling channels, giving those channels exclusive access to those seats and keeping them unavailable in the rest of the channels.\n\nℹ️Internal Presentation for the BDE (dated 08/05/2025): \nUse cases\nReserve seats for invitations: By assigning the desired seats to the “Invitation” channel, those seats will be available just for the “Invitations” section of the Fever Zone, avoiding other users to book them. This solves the previous situation where the seats must be marked as “Not for sale” to prevent marketplace users from booking the seats before the invitations are created by the partner.\nGive exclusive access to seats to different channels: The Event seats can be distributed among different whitelabels, resellers, businesses, …. This increases granularity, as the distribution can be done at seat level, compared to the current approach, that allows to do this at Session Type level.\nHow\nSeating channeling is a feature that allows to assign seats (at timeslot level) to specific selling channels, making those seats unavailable for the channels they are not assigned to.\nNote on the behavior of channel assignment\nWhen a seat is assigned to a channel it becomes available to purchase just from that channel.\nIf the seat is not assigned to any channel, it is still purchasable from any other channel.\nTherefore, a user of a given channel will only see as available the seats of its channel AND the seats without channel.\nThe following picture describes this behavior:\n*\nUser Manual\nℹ️Every step detailed in this section, apart from the GIFs that are displayed, have the complete video iin the following folder: \nAssign seats to channels\nThe channel assignment must be performed from Fever Zone, within the “Seating Management” section.\n\n*\nUnassign seats from channels\nSimilarly, seat assignment can be modified at any time, either assigning a seat to a different channel or leaving it without channel.\n\n*\nResult of the assignment\nAfter saving the assignment depicted in the previous steps, the effect is immediate, showing the seats as available just in the assigned channels.\n\n*\nLimitations\nAt the moment, we do not support the assignment of multiple channels to a given seat. For example, we could not assign a seat to two resellers at the same time, so assigning to one of them would make the seat unavailable for the other. This is mainly due to a limitation of SeatsIO.\nSimilarly, there is no support to assign seats to Channel Types (e.g., assign the seat to all channels of type Affiliate Portal).\nJust the channels in which the main plan is published are available for channel assignment (i.e., are shown in the iframe of SeatsIO inside the Fever Zone).\nIf a main plan is unpublished from a channel, the seats that were assigned to it remain unavailable on the rest of channels. In such a case, it is necessary to unassign them manually to release them for the rest of the channels.\nHow to activate\nThe functionality to carry out the channel assignment of seats is controlled by the Feature Flag show_seating_channels_edit_button. \n\nIf such Feature Flag is disabled, the button to open the assignment iframe in the “Seating Management” section will disappear. However, in case some assignment was already done, it will still be active, preventing channels from accessing seats that are not assigned to them.\n\nIn case that, for some reason, this Feature Flag is disabled for a partner and they want to revert a channel assignment, it can be done from the SeatsIO webpage (within the workspace associated with the partner) by someone with access to it (contact the Engineering Team).\n"}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.gvatx216q5zl"],"nmc":{"ty":"as","st":"document","si":0,"ei":0,"sm":{"ds_lhs":1,"ds_df":{"df_dm":1},"ds_ulhfl":false}}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.gvatx216q5zl"],"nmc":{"ty":"ae","et":"date","id":"kix.1uedsqslftu9","epm":{"dat_df":{"dfie_pt":3,"dfie_ts":{"tv":{"tv_s":1.7488656E9,"tv_n":0}},"dfie_dt":"Jun 2, 2025","dfie_l":"en","dfie_tzi":"","fres_frt":1,"dfie_p":{"tres_tv":"MMM d, Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.40z4gy1ti1ga"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"Reservation Rules\nReservation rules allow Partners to control their reservation behaviour. \nTable of contents\nTable of contents\nWhat are the Business Reservation Rules?\nCapacity limits\nMinimum tickets per reservation\nMaximum reservations per day\nTimeframe Rules\nReservations open\nReservations close\nPurchases close\nExpiration of unpaid reservations\nWhen reservations close\nHours after booking\nSetting restrictions\nBusiness type \u0027All\u0027\nSetting rules for specific business types\nDeleting custom settings for a specific business type\nAllowed values and empty configuration\nRelated fields validation\n\nWhat are the Business Reservation Rules?\nRules are restrictions that affect the Business\u0027s (Reseller) ability to make Reservations. These restrictions are used by the Self Service Model, as the Reservations will be carried out by the Businesses themselves. If not specified, restriction rules will have a set of default values specified below. We can configure the following restrictions:\nCapacity limits\nMinimum tickets per reservation\nA reservation must include at least this number of tickets.\nExample: 10 → Every reservation must include at least 10 tickets.\nDefault: 9.\nMaximum reservations per day\nThe maximum number of successful reservations a Business can make in a day.\nExample: 20 → Up to 20 reservations per day.\nDefault: 8.\n\n\u0010\u0012\u001cℹ️ Capacity can also be limited from the Fever Zone, under Events → Inventory Setup → Ticket types. This setting allows configuring how many tickets of a specific type a user can hold for a given session, including both orders (purchased tickets) and pending reservations. This option can be overridden per business type. \n\u0011\nTimeframe Rules\nReservations open\nHow far in advance of an event businesses can start making reservations.\n\nExample: 1000 → reservations open 1000 hours before the event start.\nEmpty → reservations open as soon as the event is published.\nDefault: 2160 hours (90 days).\nReservations close\nHow long before the event starts businesses stop being able to make reservations. The cutoff is always rounded to the end of that day.\n\nExample: Event at 3 PM, value 24 → cutoff would be 3 PM the previous day, then rounded to end of that previous day. Businesses could make reservations for that event until the end of the previous day. \nEmpty → reservations remain open until the end of the event’s day.\nDefault: 168 hours (7 days).\nPurchases close\nHow long before the event starts Businesses can use the “Buy now” option in the reservation flow. This cutoff is also rounded to the end of that day.\n\nExample: Event at 3 PM, value 24 → cutoff would be 3 PM the previous day, then rounded to end of that previous day. Businesses could make purchases until the end of the previous day\nEmpty → purchases remain open until the end of the event’s day.\nDefault: 24 hours.\nExpiration of unpaid reservations\nControls when an unpaid reservation expires. When a reservation expires, it is cancelled and its reserved capacity is freed for other purchases.\n\nThis setting has two options:\nWhen reservations close\nThis is the default behavior. All unpaid reservations will expire when the \"Reservations close\" cutoff is reached. Their reserved capacity is freed for other purchases at that time.\nHours after booking\nThis option allows you to set a specific time limit for payment after a reservation is created.\n\nHours: Enter the number of hours a Business has to pay for their reservation after creating it.\nExample: 48 → A Business has 48 hours from the time they make a reservation to complete the payment. If they do not pay within this timeframe, the reservation will expire and the capacity will be released.\nNote: The effective expiration will always be the earliest between this rule and the \"Reservations close\" date. For example, if \"Hours\" is set to 48, but a Business makes a reservation 3 hours before the \"Reservations close\" cutoff, that reservation will expire in 3 hours, not 48.\nSetting restrictions\nThe restrictions are set at the Partner level and can also be set at the Business Type level (channel). For example, Business Type Educational can have different restrictions than Agency
