Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.40z4gy1ti1ga"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":4154,"s":"\u000bYou can find the reservation rules configuration screen under the Reservations menu, Rules sub-menu.\u000b\n*\nBusiness type \u0027All\u0027\nThe screen has two columns. On the left, we can select a business type, while the right column shows us the different settings that we can define. \u000bBy default, there are predefined values for every setting that apply to every business type.\u000b \n*\nSetting rules for specific business types\nBy selecting the business type on the dropdown you can check whether custom rules exist or default rules apply.\n\nWhen the Custom rules toggle is off, it means that default rules are applied, and the values are shown grayed out. This is by design so that values that apply to every business type can only be changed when Business type All is selected in the dropdown. \u000b\n*\n\nTo change settings for the specific business type selected in the drop-down menu, you must turn the Custom rules toggle on.\n\nIn the image below, we can see how we removed limitations for every setting by leaving them blank only for Business type Agency, and by pressing save we see a confirmation message on the top right corner. This confirmation means the changes have been applied and take immediate effect. \n\n\n*\nDeleting custom settings for a specific business type\nBy turning Custom rules toggle off, you are effectively deleting the customization for the selected business type. Values defined in the \u0027All\u0027 section are applied instead. You can see them greyed out. It\u0027s important to press the Save button for the changes to take effect, and it will be confirmed by a green message in the top right corner.\n\n*\nAllowed values and empty configuration\nValues for every setting must be defined in numbers and be greater than zero, or they can be left empty. They can never be defined using words or using the number 0, otherwise, an error message is displayed below the non-compliant fields.\n\n*\n\nAs mentioned before It\u0027s possible to leave one, many or all fields empty, and it will be shown as a correct configuration as displayed in the image below.\n\n*\nRelated fields validation\nDue to the relationship between \"Reservations open\", \"Reservations close\" and “Purchases close” the system will validate them together, to make sure that\n\n“Reservations open” is either greater than \"Reservations close\" and  \"Purchases close\" or empty (“no limits”)\n“Reservations close” is either greater than “Purchases close” and lower than “Reservations open” or empty (“At the end of the event day”)\n“Purchases close is either lower than “Reservations open” and “Reservations close”  or empty (no limits) \n\u000bOnce purchases close, businesses won’t be able to make reservations, even if “Reservations close” is left empty. \nIf this is misconfigured by mistake, the validation will inform you, and you won\u0027t be able to save the configuration until the values are fixed. Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.une0zbu3iqcx"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"Business Registration API integration\nThe process to integrate the Business creation with an external Form.\nBase URL\nPath\nBody\nExample\nCommon errors\n\nBase URL\nThese are the base urls that will be used to send the data to each environment. For testing, if you are able to run the b2b-iam project in your local machine, we recommend you to use the local base URL. But, if that’s not the case, you can also use the staging base URL. And then, set the prod base URL for the prod configuration.\n \nPROD\nhttps://services.feverup.com/b2b-iam\n\nSTAGING\nhttps://services.staging.feverup.com/b2b-iam\n\nLOCAL\nhttp://localhost:8003/b2b-iam\nPath\nPOST - /1.0/organizations/{partnerId}/businesses\n\npartnerId: Organization where the businesses will be created. For Staging and Local you can use 198 as partner ID. For Production, ask the manager for the partner ID of the correct Organization.\nBody\nThe body that should be sent in the POST should contain some required fields and some specific validations. \n\nThese are the required fields that should be sent always:\n\n - name\n - email\n - phone\n - contact_name\n - business_type\n \nThese are all the fields that are accepted, along with all the specifications and validations that they have. The ones with an * are the required fields.\n\nname*\nBusiness\u0027 name\nexample: Raquel\nvalidators\nMin length 4 characters\nemail*\nBusiness\u0027 email\nexample: raquel@test.com\nvalidators:\nPattern ^(?:[a-zA-Z0-9!#$%\u0026\"*+\\\u003d?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\\.[a-zA-Z0-9!#$%\u0026\"*+\\\u003d?^_`{|}~-]+)*)@(?:(?:[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?\\.)+[a-zA-Z0-9](?:[a-zA-Z0-9-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])+)$\nbusiness_type*\nBusiness\u0027 type\nexample: educational\naccepted values:\nagency\neducational\ncultural\nguide\ncorporate\nlarge_group\npremium_services\nphone*\nBusiness\u0027 Phone number\nexample: +34612345654\nvalidators\nMin length 3 characters\nMax length 6 characters\ncontact_name*\nBusiness\u0027 contact name\nexample: Rhonda Gonzalez\nvalidators\nMin length 2 characters\ntax_identification_number\nBusiness\u0027 tax identification number\nexample: X12345678A\nvalidators\nMin length 3 characters\naddress\nBusiness\u0027 address\nexample: Calle Gran Via\nvalidators\nMin length 3 characters\ncity_code\nBusiness\u0027 city code\nexample: ‘123453X’\nvalidators\nMin length 3 characters\ncountry_code\nBusiness\u0027 country code\nexample: ES\nvalidators\nMust be one of these valid country ISO A2 Codes https://www.rocketroute.com/iso-country-code-list\npostal_code\nBusiness\u0027 postal code\nexample: 1234X\nbilling_name\nBusiness\u0027 billing name\nexample: Guru walk\nvalidators\nMin length 2 characters\neducational_group_of_eu_or_latam\nIf business is an educational group of eu or latam\nexample: false\naccepted_terms\nBusiness\u0027 accepted terms\nexample: [\"tos-25052018\", \"privacy-25052018\"]\nis_manager\n If the business created is manager with permissions to create reservations or not \nexample: true\nlanguage\nPreferred language for communications with business\nexample: es_ES\nExample\nExample of the payload that will be accepted by the server with the fields filled:\n\n{\n   “email”: `raquel@test.com`,\n   “tax_identification_number”: `123456789X`,\n   “name”: `Name` ,\n   “billing_name”: `billing name`,\n   “phone”: `+34123456789`,\n   “contact_name”: `Contact name`,\n   “business_type”: `educational`,\n   “address”: `Calle Prueba, 0`,\n   “city_code”: `1111`,\n   “postal_code”: `012345`,\n   “receive_information”: true,\n   “accepted_terms”: [\"tos-25052018\", \"privacy-25052018\"],\n   “is_manager”: true,\n   “country_code”: `ES`,\n   “language”: `es_ES`\n};\nCommon errors\n\nYou are trying to create a Business with an email that already exists on the system. \n\u000bFor example, test@test.com already has a business account so, if you try to Register again with this email, you will see a generic error message. Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.vi38llbf3dle"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"Events - WIP\nThis section defines the events for the Reservations flow in Resellers.\nIt lists event names, required properties, and when they fire, so Product\u0026Engineering can track success and failure consistently across the flow.\nSúper props - WIP\nStable, session-scoped attributes that must accompany every reservation event.\nThey are registered when the flow starts and updated only when their value becomes known (After selecting a business, event, venue, or session).\n\nReservationID: {id}|null\nCartId: {id}|null\nFlowType: new|upgrade\nPartnerID: {id}\nBookingAgentID: {id}|null\nEventID: {id}|null\nCityId: {id}\nVenueId: {id}\nCurrency\n\nOverall flow events - WIP\nScreen-agnostic, principal milestones of the reservation funnel that may happen on different screens but keep the same meaning (CommitAttempt, Created, StatusChange, Expired).\nUse these events as the source of truth for funnel and lifecycle metrics, independent of UI changes.\nFz|Resellers|Reservations|Created\nThis event is triggered when the reservation confirmation (2xx) is received from the backend.\n\nCommitEntryPoint: recipient_details|confirmation_modal\nHasOverride: True|False\nOverrideType: fixed|percentaje|null\nOverrideOperation: add|reduce|null\nRealValue: {Qty} //Real value without override\nOverrideAmount: {Qty}|Null\nRoundingAmount: {Qty}|Null\nOutstandingAmount: Qty\nItemsCount: {Qty}\nreservationExpiresAt: expirationDate\u0026Time\n\nFz|Resellers|Reservations|CommitAttempt\nEmitted whenever the user (or client code) initiates a reservation commit from any UI surface.\nIt fires before any backend request. A cart may produce one or more attempts.\n\nAttemptEntryPoint: recipient_details|confirmation_modal\nHasOverride: True|False\n\n\nOverview screen - WIP\nBooking Agent Selection screen - WIP\nTicket selection screen - WIP\nRecipient details screen - WIP\nOverride module - WIP\nFz|Resellers|Reservations|Override|Toggle\n\nThis event is triggered every time a user activates the switch that enables the Override module.\u000b\nNewState: True|False\nScope: recipient_details //To be reviewed\n\n*\nFz|Resellers|Reservations|Override|RoundingHelp|Click\nThis event is triggered every time a user clicks on the “What\u0027s that?” link in the Rounding section within the Override module.\u000b\nScope: recipient_details //To be reviewed\nRounding_amount: Qty\n\n*\n\nConfirmation modal - Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.7ab0epp23egt"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"Time Slot Booking (TSB)\nAllowing partners to block the full session when the first comes\nLatest version: \n\n\u0010\u0012\u001cℹ️ Please, if you find any mistakes, have suggestions, or have any questions, add a comment assigned to \n\u0011Table of Contents\nTable of Contents\nAbstract\nGlossary of terms\nTime Slot Booking\nExclusivity Models / Use Cases\nTechnical documentation\nAbstract\nThe Time Slot Booking system guarantees the exclusivity of the booked experiences. Designed for activities such as escape room sessions and museum visits, the system ensures that once a reservation or order is made, the entire time slot is exclusively reserved for that group, regardless of who books it.\n\nThis means that the complete experience at that specific hour is blocked, preventing any other user from making a reservation for the same time slot. For example, for an event on 12/08/2025 from 5:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m. and a combination of tickets, when it is booked, then, others can not book the same day, hour regardless of the tickets. \n\nNow, there are two main strategies of booking, taking into account: 1) orders and reservations or 2) reservations to determine if a time slot is sold out.\n\nAdditionally, once a reservation/order is done, the system automatically marks the all associated sessions for that slot to Sold Out, avoiding in the User Interface the purchase.\n\nGlossary of terms\nSpeaking the same language is important to fully understand the system and its capabilities. Here are the commonly used terms and definitions:\nTime Slot – A designated period during which an experience can be booked, i.e, a day in an hour and a combination of tickets for that time.\nExclusivity – A combined mechanism that, once a booking (order or reservation) is made, reserves the entire time slot exclusively. This process prevents any further bookings within that slot.\nSold Out – A status indicating that a session is fully booked and no longer available for additional reservations or orders.\nChannel – The platform where the purchase takes place (e.g., Fever website, resellers in FZ).\nTickets – Options that you can select when purchasing an event, the possible options could be “Adults”, “Kids”, “2 Adults 1 Kid” and similar.\nTime Slot Booking Capacity – The TSB allows the possibility to make n reservations/orders in a timeslot determined by the capacity of the time slot booking capacity. For instance, for an event on 12/08/2025 from 5:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m. and a config of tickets when it is reserved, no other can reserve if the capacity is set to 1. However, if the capacity is set to 2, then it lets another customer book the same event, at the same time and the config of tickets the customer wants.\nTime Slot Booking\nExclusivity Models / Use Cases\nNow, we have two main streams: CAPN (Patrimonio Nacional) and other partners.\nB2B Reservations (CAPN): Allows groups to register as resellers in their web.\nExclusivity:\nThose resellers (school groups, travel agencies…) are allowed to make reservations from the FeverZone (FZ). When one of those resellers books a slot in the FZ (an event, x day at y time and a config of tickets), then no other resellers from the FZ will be able to book the same timeslot.\nFlexibility:\nHowever, from the Marketplace channel (feverup.com webpage) and Box-Office channel, tickets for that same event, day and hour (i.e. Time Slot) can still be purchased, if the current purchases do not exceed the capacity of the plan. This means that in those channels purchases don’t have the exclusivity restrictions.\nSold-out system:\nAll related sessions published with channel type: b2b_portal, such as all the reseller channels, for that slot are immediately marked as Sold Out for other FZ resellers when the total number of bookings exceed the time slot booking capacity.\nAs it is only for reservations, only the channels that can make reservations will be taken into account for Sold Out computation.\nExample:\nA school that registers as a reseller and books a museum at x timeslot. So, other resellers will not be able to book the same slot in FZ, but in the Marketplace channel and Box-Office channel there may be single customers who enter together with the school at the same event and time.\n"}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.7ab0epp23egt"],"nmc":{"ty":"as","st":"document","si":0,"ei":0,"sm":{"ds_lhs":1,"ds_df":{"df_dm":1},"ds_ulhfl":false}}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.7ab0epp23egt"],"nmc":{"ty":"ae","et":"list","id":"kix.73yb4pnyzzdn","epm":{"le_nb":{"nl_4":{"b_gf":"","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":162.0,"b_gs":"","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":1,"b_il":180.0},"nl_3":{"b_gf":"","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":126.0,"b_gs":"","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":0,"b_il":144.0},"nl_2":{"b_gf":"","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":90.0,"b_gs":"","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":2,"b_il":108.0},"nl_1":{"b_gf":"","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd": books the selected session, then other resellers can not book that session. But in the Marketplace or Box-office, we could purchase a session in the same Time Slot that is not available from the FeverZone. Now, this event only has the ticket “Tarifa Básica”, but if it had more, when the school books the “Tarifa Básica”, all tickets (including “Tarifa Básica”) could not be reserved.\n*\nResume schema for purchases when time slot booking capacity is exceeded:\n*\nOrders + Reservations (e.g.: Sensas, Fever Originals, Tyffon, Quiz Room…): Resellers are just internal workers, using FeverZone as their own \"backoffice\".\nExclusivity:\nWhen a customer buys in the Marketplace channel and Affiliates channels, the slot must stay blocked. Other clients from the Marketplace channel and Affiliates channels will NOT be able to make purchases for the same slot.\nFlexibility:\nResellers, which in this case are internal workers, will be able to make orders/reservations and add users to the timeslot from the FZ or Box-Office channel, even if it has already been booked from the Marketplace channel.\nSold-out system:\nAll related sessions published with channel type: marketplace, api and affiliate_portal for that slot are immediately marked as Sold Out for other customers in Marketplace and Affiliate channels when the total number of bookings exceed the time slot booking capacity.\nAs it is for orders and reservations, bookings in any channel will be taken into account for Sold Out computation.\nExample:\nA customer buys 4 tickets for an escape room, but calls Sensas to change to 6 people. Then, Sensas adds two more people in FZ and sends the order to be paid to the customer. If the time slot booking capacity is 1, no other customers in Marketplace or the Affiliate channels could purchase the same slot. The same if you want to add when you are at the box office, you are allowed to add orders to the same event, day and time.\nResume schema for purchases when time slot booking capacity is exceeded:\n*\n\nLimitations / sold out lifecycle\nIt is important to note that the update of the Sold Out status is not retroactive. This means that if a slot was already purchased and new sessions are added afterward, those new sessions will not be automatically marked as Sold Out.\nTo mitigate this issue, two new mechanisms have been introduced:\nRecurring Groups Update:\nWhen new recurring groups are added, the system will attempt to normalize and update the Sold Out status for all sessions that have bookings.\nRecurringSessionsCreationFinishedEvent: This is an event that gets called when there is a bulk insert of sessions, normally done by the operations team. This happens when, for example, there is a newly created session type for a mainplan and therefore, timeslots that have been marked as Sold Out by TSB should have this new session marked as Sold Out as well. In this case, the system searches such sessions and updates the Sold Out status for all sessions that have been blocked by TSB.\nRecalculation Button in BO:\nA button has been incorporated into the Back Office (BO) for each main plan that recalculates the Sold Out status for all the sessions of the plan that have orders, updating each session. This is used in situations where the event is not called and therefore the status of the newly created session type is not updated automatically or when we are in an inconsistent Sold Out status and we want to synchronize the correct status.\n\nNote that if we insert a new session in BO it is not marked as Sold Out and is who created the new session the one charged to mark it if needed.\n\nRestrictions\nDo not enable TSB for Wait List plans ahead of time. If TSB is enabled for Wait Lists, it will limit the number of registrants based on the predefined capacity. Even if you plan to include TSB in the actual plan, enabling it early for Wait Lists will cause limitations that you most likely don\u0027t want yet.\n-\nTechnical Documentation\nYou can find technical details of this implementation and how to configure and create TSBs here.\n"}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.7ab0epp23egt"],"nmc":{"ty":"ae","et":"inline","id":"kix.fo2aoln7zzu2","epm":{"ee_eo":{"eo_bo":{"ln_s":0,"ln_w":0.0,"ln_c2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0}},"i_pid":null,"eo_mb":9.0,"i_ht":448.5,"eo_rtdf":{"rdf_ft":0},"eo_ml":9.0,"eo_mr":9.0,"eo_ad":null,"i_cid":"s-blob-v1-IMAGE-r0x5aEryiRA","i_cont":0.0,"eo_mt":9.0,"eo_type":0,"eo_lco":{"lc_oi":"","lc_cs":"","lc_ct":0,"lc_sci":"","lc_srk":""},"i_wth":869.25,"eo_at":null,"i_iwc":{"iwc_w":false},"eo_hb":false,"eo_rtd":"","i_crop":{"crop_oxr":0.0,"crop_oyr":0.0,"crop_rot":0.0,"crop_hr":1.0,"crop_wr":1.0},"i_iw":false,"i_rot":0.0,"i_clst":{"cv":{"op":"set","opValue":[]}},"i_bri":0.0,"i_msct":0,"i_src":"","i_opa":1.0}}}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.7ab0epp23egt"],"nmc":{"ty":"ae","et":"inline","id":"kix.fx11do2pz8yk","epm":{"ee_eo":{"eo_bo":{"ln_s":0,"ln_w":0.0,"ln_c2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0}},"i_pid":null,"eo_mb":9.0,"i_ht":279.5482666015623,"eo_rtdf":{"rdf_ft":0},"eo_ml":9.0,"eo_mr":9.0,"eo_ad":null,"i_cid":"s-blob-v1-IMAGE-lLP1JTb8CuA","i_cont":0.0,"eo_mt":9.0,"eo_type":0,"eo_lco":{"lc_oi":"","lc_cs":"","lc_ct":0,"lc_sci":"","lc_srk":""},"i_wth":467.369758224487,"eo_at":null,"i_iwc":{"iwc_w":false},"eo_hb":false,"eo_rtd":"","i_crop":{"crop_oxr":0.0,"crop_oyr":0.0,"crop_rot":0.0,"crop_hr":1.0,"crop_wr":1.0},"i_iw":false,"i_rot":0.0,"i_clst":{"cv":{"op":"set","opValue":[]}},"i_bri":0.0,"i_msct":0,"i_src":"","i_opa":1.0}}}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.7ab0epp23egt"],"nmc":{"ty":"ae","et":"inline","id":"kix.jfroboqs3g89","epm":{"ee_eo":{"eo_bo":{"ln_s":0,"ln_w":0.0,"ln_c2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0}},"i_pid":null,"eo_mb":9.0,"i_ht":479.25,"eo_rtdf":{"rdf_ft":0},"eo_ml":9.0,"eo_mr":9.0,"eo_ad":null,"i_cid":"s-blob-v1-IMAGE-3S1R_ZDAMZc","i_cont":0.0,"eo_mt":9.0,"eo_type":0,"eo_lco":{"lc_oi":"","lc_cs":"","lc_ct":0,"lc_sci":"","lc_srk":""},"i_wth":867.75,"eo_at":null,"i_iwc":{"iwc_w":false},"eo_hb":false,"eo_rtd":"","i_crop":{"crop_oxr":0.0,"crop_oyr":0.0,"cr Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.pf8y89ny8pvk"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"Reseller research questionnaire\nList of questions to ask the partner to get a good understanding of how they operate at the reseller level\nLatest version \n\nThis document aims to engage in a pre-demo conversation to understand how they operate and where they want to go by implementing the Fever solution. This set of questions, oriented towards how our system works, will help us determine which implementation of our functionalities is best.\n🧩 Collaboration Model \u0026 Objectives\nWhat is the main objective of the Partner when working with us? (e.g., increase sales, optimize group bookings, create affiliate networks, etc.)\nDo they currently work with agencies, institutions, or groups?\nWhat type of commercial relationships do they want to enable?\nGroups / Collectives (B2B)\nAffiliates / Ambassadors (B2C promotional)\nOTAs / Platforms (e.g., Viator, Tiqets…)\nHow do they usually define the nature of these relationships? (pre-negotiated agreements or not)\nWhat level of autonomy do they want to give third parties for managing reservations?\nFull autonomy (Self-service model)\nPartial control (manual review or approval)\nNo autonomy (Partner manages everything directly)\nHow are partner–reseller agreements typically established?\n🧾 Group Booking Management\nDo they currently sell to groups? How do they manage this today?\nWhat type of entities usually buy group tickets from them? (e.g., schools, businesses, travel agencies, cultural groups…)\nWhat is the average group reservation volume they manage?\nDo they accept payments via bank transfer or only by card?\nDo they need to allow partial payments (e.g., deposits)?\nDo they have specific rules for group bookings? (e.g., minimum 10 people, 7-day advance booking…)\n💼 Customer Types \u0026 Segmentation\nDo they want to segment prices or booking rules based on the type of customer? (e.g., agencies, educational institutions, premium services…)\nDo they want to customize prices per channel, customer type, or time slot?\nDo they have different capacity rules or booking restrictions per sales channel?\n🔑 Roles \u0026 System Access\nWho in their organization will manage group reservations?\nDo they need differentiated roles? (e.g., Admin, Reservation Manager, Box Office)\nDo they want private/independent access for third parties (e.g., agencies, schools)?\nDo they want to pre-approve which entities can make reservations?\n💳 Payments \u0026 Reconciliation\nWho should collect the money? Fever or the Partner?\nAre they comfortable with card payments as the main method?\nWould they like to offer credit/balance to trusted buyers?\nDo they already have reconciliation processes with their resellers or agencies?\n🧠 Technical \u0026 Operational Needs\nDo they need a landing page where third parties can register automatically?\nDo they want to integrate with their system via API?\nDo they need automatic invoicing capabilities?\nDo they need reports or dashboards to track performance?\n📣 Promotions \u0026 Affiliates\nDo they work with affiliates, influencers, or brand ambassadors?\nDo they want to offer private promotions for specific groups or company employees?\nDo they need to track sales per affiliate/network?\nAre they interested in gamification or a points-based rewards system?\n📅 Restrictions \u0026 Booking Rules\nDo they want to block entire time slots once a group reservation is made? (TSB)\nDo they have strict availability rules?\nWhat are their current policies for cancellations or rescheduling?\n⚠️ Special Limitations or Conditions\nAre there any operational, legal, or technical limitations we should know about?\nDo they currently face problems with their booking or reseller system?\nHave they had negative experiences with similar solutions in the past?\n📌 Post-Meeting Actions\nDocument the most appropriate model (Self-Service, Partner Mode, Hybrid)\nIdentify needs for manual activation or future development\nPropose a demo tailored to their specific use case\nLog DOCS_warmStartDocumentLoader.loadModelChunk(DOCS_modelChunk); DOCS_modelChunk = undefined;</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunkParseStart = new Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.lfta2ifd5yff"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"(WIP) Affiliates, ambassadors \u0026 public relationships platform\nThe feature requires a lot of manual operation and is not ready to be scaled up to large levels.\nConsult with the Product Manager about the tool\u0027s capabilities, the cases we are currently covering, and its limitations. Only offer it to customers who understand the current status of the tool.\n\nConfluence documentation  \nTechnical presentation of Affiliates \nWhy\nAffiliate marketing is a strategy in which a company compensates third-party publishers, called affiliates, to generate traffic or leads to the company’s products and services. For that, partners must track affiliate sales and provide them with tools to sell their inventory.\n\nPartners rely on influencers, ambassadors, public relationships, affiliates, and referrals to drive sales, typically compensating them with an incentive for each sale. To support this Fever built a system that allows the creation of tracked specific websites on top of the Event, called B2C Sites, that can show different prices, tickets or descriptions of the Plan. Each B2C site has a tracking code that attributes each sale to the affiliate assigned. Partners can assign a specific B2C page with discounts to build promotional campaigns or attribute affiliation networks.\n\nThis capability is particularly interesting for partners offering nightlife experiences, concerts, and festivals, where affiliation sales are important.\nWe have 2 tools to differentiate:\nThe Ambassador Program Tool\nThe Affiliate or Public Relationship Tool\nThe Ambassador Program\nAmbassadors is a spinoff of the Affiliates tool that provides a comprehensive, gamified solution for partners to leverage sales through ambassadors. It offers several key functionalities:\n\nEffortless Registration:\nA point of registration to a specific event via our public portal, ensuring low friction and quick participation.\nUser/Partner-Specific Ambassador Link:\nUpon registration, each ambassador receives a unique personal link (affiliate_code) tied to both their user account and the partner they represent. This link drives and tracks sales accurately.\nGamified Points-Counting System:\nAmbassadors start collecting points with every ticket sale driven through their link, creating a foundation for future rewards and incentivizing active promotion.\nRedemption System:\nA mechanism to exchange accumulated points for prizes or rewards, completing the gamification loop.\nAdditional Context \u0026 Opportunity:\nIndustry Relevance:\nParticularly valuable for partners in the music and nightlife sectors (e.g., Last Tour, Kapital), this solution meets the demand for engaging, data-driven ambassador programs.\nScalable \u0026 Data-Driven:\nThe system not only tracks performance but also provides a scalable approach to monitor engagement and drive revenue.\nNext Steps:\nImplement Points Redemption:\nThe upcoming phase involves integrating a robust points redemption system to further incentivize ambassadors and complete the gamification cycle.\nYou can refer to a concise presentation that offers comprehensive details about the registration process. \n\nBenefits\nThis tool introduces new channels of revenue source for partners, especially those who currently lack a way to track affiliates or never used them.\nBroader market: Introduces new sources of revenue leveraging the creation of affiliate networks and properly tracking them.\nBetter resource spending: The partner can monitor affiliate sales making it easier to give accurate benefits based on data and performance.\nUse Cases\nA concert organizer partners with multiple influencers to promote an upcoming music festival and needs a way to track and reward them. They create and manage their influencers inside Fever Zone.\nA theatre company wants to boost ticket sales for a new play by partnering with local businesses. The company creates a branded website for local businesses to sell discounted tickets.\nA nightclub wants to leverage influencers and promoters to drive ticket sales for its weekly events. The company manages its networks of affiliates and sub-affiliates inside Fever Zone and incentivizes them according to their sales.\n"}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.lfta2ifd5yff"],"nmc":{"ty":"as","st":"document","si":0,"ei":0,"sm":{"ds_lhs":1,"ds_df":{"df_dm":1},"ds_ulhfl":false}}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.lfta2ifd5yff"],"nmc":{"ty":"ae","et":"first-party-link","id":"kix.3757kxk90qzd","epm":{"fple-u":"https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1eW70HzTJB4v9ZlwJeQc-T5iDOBT-8cR4S4XLBwRGX5I/edit#slide\u003did.g2f06c21d868_0_134","fple-t":"Affiliation - B2C Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.lfta2ifd5yff"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":4148,"s":"How\nThe tool relies on B2C sites, which are replicas of the plan but with some personalisation elements that refer to the affiliate.\nUser Manual\nCreate an Affiliate\nB2C sites are websites with plans associated with these affiliates, where you can view the plans and execute those purchases. Stakeholders can also be companies or individuals. A very clear example is public relations who sell tickets for a disco or band. Stakeholders can also be companies that resell plans. An example is travel agencies. They can even be companies that want to offer discounts to their employees as a benefit. The point is that behind a B2C there is a stakeholder interested in receiving something in return for selling a partner\u0027s plans. It can be reputation, money or profit. In short, they are just another sales channel that has certain needs.\n\nRight now the process is very manual. To create affiliates Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.g7h9ejghpdon"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"Ambassador Program Management Guide\nConfluence documentation \nCreation of an Ambassador Program\n\nTo create an Ambassador Program, we can use the partner ambassador program creation endpoint in Postman logging in as the desired partner:\n\nPOST b2b-partners/1.0/partners/{partner_id}/ambassador-programs\n\nAccepted fields for the request body:\n\n\u0010\u0012\u001c\n\u001cField\n\u001cType\n\u001cExample\n\u0012\u001cMandatory\n\u001ctitle\n\u001cstring\n\u001c“Example ambassador program”\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cdescription\n\u001cstring\n\u001c“\u003cp\u003eExample description\u003c/p\u003e”\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cstarts_at\n\u001cdate in ISO 8601\n\u001c\"2025-12-31T23:59:59+00:00\"\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cends_at\n\u001cdate in ISO 8601\n\u001c\"2026-12-31T23:59:59+00:00\"\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001credeem_expiration_at\n\u001cdate in ISO 8601\n\u001c\"2026-12-31T23:59:59+00:00\"\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cgdpr_text\n\u001cstring\n\u001c\"By subbiting this form you accept…\"\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cplans\n\u001clist of plan_ids\n\u001c[123, 345]\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001ctranslations\n\u001clist (can be empty)\n\u001c[]\n\u0012\u001cOptional\n\u001cimage\n\u001cstring, url\n\u001c“https://example.com/image.png”\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cterms_and_conditions_link\n\u001cstring, url\n\u001c\"https://example.com/terms\"\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cprivacy_policies_link\n\u001cstring, url\n\u001c\"https://example.com/privacy\"\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001ccontact_support_email\n\u001cstring, email\n\u001c\"support@example.com\"\n\u0011\nOptional fields not provided will be treated as Null, except translations, which will be treated as an empty list.\n\n⚠️Special considerations:\nTitle must be unique between the ambassador programs of each partner, i.e. a partner can’t have two ambassador programs with the same title.\nFor styling the description, it needs to be introduced in HTML format.\nRegarding time: Both ends_at and redeem_expiration_at must be later than starts_at\nplans is a list containing at least 1 plan for the current partner.\nA plan can only be in an ambassador program, even when the AP is deleted\n‼️Currently, an ambassador program can only have 1 plan while we refactor the registration process to work with ambassador programs instead of plans.\n\nKeep in mind that the fields filled will be shown in the registration page as follows:\n*\n\nGeneric description:\n\n\u003ch3\u003eJoin the Ambassador Program and Unlock Exclusive Rewards!\u003c/h3\u003e \u003cp\u003eBecome an ambassador for your favorite events and enjoy exciting benefits:\u003c/p\u003e\u003cul\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eTrack Your Progress:\u003c/strong\u003e See your earned points and monitor your performance by event.\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eEarn Rewards:\u003c/strong\u003e Redeem your points for exclusive prizes.\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eBoost Your Impact:\u003c/strong\u003e The more tickets you sell, the more rewards you unlock.\u003c/li\u003e\u003c/ul\u003e\u003cp\u003eWith a gamified points system, you’ll stay motivated while connecting others to unforgettable experiences. Join now and start earning!\u003c/p\u003e\n\nGeneric GDPR text:\n\nBy submitting this form, you agree that Fever Labs Inc. will process your personal data to manage your request. You can exercise your rights by contacting us at dpo@feverup.com.\n\nEdition of an Ambassador Program\nTo edit an existing ambassador program, we can use the partner ambassador program edit endpoint. It accepts the same fields as the creation one, but in this case none of the fields is mandatory. \n\n⚠️The fields sent in the body will replace the existing values, while not specifying a field will leave it unchanged from its current value.\n\nThis is especially important for translations, since the list of translations sent will replace the existing one, this means that if there are existing translations but a body is sent with a list of translations that doesn’t include the existing ones, those will be deleted. Thus, for editing a translation from the endpoint, the body needs to be sent with the full list of translations.\nAmbassador Program Translations\nTranslations are needed to correctly display the ambassador program information in each language. The following fields can be translated:\n\n\u0010\u0012\u001c\n\u001cField\n\u001cType\n\u001cExample\n\u0012\u001cMandatory\n\u001clanguage\n\u001cstring\n\u001c\"Es\", ”es-ES”, “en”, “en-US”\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001ctitle\n\u001cstring\n\u001c“Translated title”\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cdescription\n\u001cstring\n\u001c“\u003cp\u003eTranslated description\u003c/p\u003e”\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cgdpr_text\n\u001cstring\n\u001c“Translated GDPR text”\n\u0012\u001cOptional\n\u001cterms_and_conditions_link\n\u001cstring, url\n\u001c“https://example.es/terms”\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cprivacy_policies_link\n\u001cstring, url\n\u001c“https://feverup.es/privacy”\n\u0011"}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.g7h9ejghpdon"],"nmc":{"ty":"as","st":"document","si":0,"ei":0,"sm":{"ds_lhs":1,"ds_df":{"df_dm":1},"ds_ulhfl":false}}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.g7h9ejghpdon"],"nmc":{"ty":"ae","et":"list","id":"kix.fpghs6y6fiak","epm":{"le_nb":{"nl_4":{"b_gf":"%4","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":162.0,"b_gs":"○","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":9,"b_il":180.0},"nl_3":{"b_gf":"%3","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":126.0,"b_gs":"●","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":9,"b_il":144.0},"nl_2":{"b_gf":"%2","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_st":false,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fgc2_i":true,"ts_sc_i":true,"ts_tw":400},"b_ifl":90.0,"b_gs":"■","b_a":0,"b_sn":1,"b_gt":9,"b_il":108.0},"nl_1":{"b_gf":"%1","b_ts":{"ts_un":false,"ts_un_i":false,"ts_sc":false,"ts_it_i":true,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#000000","clr_type":0},"ts_st_i":true,"ts_bgc2":{"hclr_color":null,"clr_type":0},"ts_fs_i":true,"ts_ff_i":true,"ts_bgc2_i":true,"ts_it":false,"ts_va":"nor","ts_bd_i":true,"ts_va_i":true, Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.g7h9ejghpdon"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":4025,"s":"\nTranslations are essential to display the Ambassador Program registration page in multiple languages, title, description and GDPR text are mandatory and always translated. \n\nTerms and conditions link and privacy policies link can be “translated” if the partner has its links available in multiple languages. If it doesn’t, the original links will show if they exist.\n\nThey can be added through the ambassador programs creation and edition endpoints as objects within the translations list.\n\nPartner endpoints will return ambassador programs untranslated, and will include a list of available translations on endpoints that create, modify or get a single program.\n\nFor non-partner endpoints, translations will be applied to the ambassador program if available for the desired language.\n\nAmbassador program registration\nTo enable registration for ambassadors on the ambassador program, we need to add and publish a registration channel as described in the first step of this guide .\n\n⚠️This process is tied to the plan assigned to the ambassador program, and it will change when ambassador programs are allowed to manage more than one plan.\n\nAfter publishing the registration channel, ambassadors will be able to register making use of the ambassador program’s public registration page.\n\nExample registration page: https://partners.feverup.com/ambassador-programs/ea3276ec-7dd4-40a8-b76e-3e2a772b7c6c/register \n\n*\nTrying to register outside of the program’s availability period will show a disabled form.\n*\n\nCreation of rewards\nTo create a Reward, we can use the partner ambassador program reward creation endpoint in Postman logging in as the desired partner using the ambassador_program_id of the program we want to create the reward into:\n\nAccepted fields for the request body:\n\n\u0010\u0012\u001c\n\u001cField\n\u001cType\n\u001cDefault value\n\u0012\u001cMandatory\n\u001ctitle\n\u001cstring\n\u001c-\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cpoints\n\u001cinteger\n\u001c-\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001ctranslations\n\u001clist (can be empty)\n\u001c-\n\u0012\u001cOptional\n\u001cimage\n\u001cstring, url\n\u001cNone\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cdescription\n\u001cstring\n\u001cNone\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cdisplay_real_availability\n\u001cbool\n\u001cTrue\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cmax_uses_per_user\n\u001cinteger\n\u001c1\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cinitial_stock\n\u001cinteger\n\u001c0\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001credeem_instructions_link\n\u001cstring, url\n\u001cNone\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001ctype\n\u001cstring\n\u001cCode\n\u0012\u001cMandatory if type is Coupon\n\u001csession_id\n\u001cinteger\n\u001cNone\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001cplan_id\n\u001cinteger\n\u001cNone\n\u0011\nOptional fields not provided will be treated as the default value.\nEdition of rewards\nTo edit an existing reward, we can use the partner ambassador program rewards edit endpoint. It accepts the same fields as the creation one, but in this case none of the fields is mandatory. \n\n⚠️The fields sent in the body will replace the existing values, while not specifying a field will leave it unchanged from its current value.\n\nThis is especially important for translations, since the list of translations sent will replace the existing one, this means that if there are existing translations but a body is sent with a list of translations that doesn’t include the existing ones, those will be deleted. Thus, for editing a translation from the endpoint, the body needs to be sent with the full list of translations.\nRewards translations\nTranslations are needed to correctly display the rewards in each language. The following fields can be translated:\n\n\u0010\u0012\u001c\n\u001cField\n\u001cType\n\u001cExample\n\u0012\u001cMandatory\n\u001clanguage\n\u001cstring\n\u001c\"es\", ”es-ES”, “en”, “en-US”\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001ctitle\n\u001cstring\n\u001c“Name of the reward”\n\u0012\u001cOptional\n\u001cdescription\n\u001cstring\n\u001c“Description of the reward in detail”\n\u0012\u001c\n\u001credeem_instructions_link\n\u001cstring, url\n\u001c“https://example.es/terms”\n\u0011\nReward translations work like ambassador program translations, updating mandatory fields and optional fields if they are not None in the translation, in which case they leave the original value of the field they translate.\n\nRewards translations can be managed through the creation and edition endpoints for ambassador program rewards.\n\nPartner endpoints will return rewards untranslated, and with a translations list if they create, update or retrieve a single reward.\n\nFor non-partner endpoints, translations will be applied if they exist for the desired available\nWhy\nThe B2B marketplace will allow Partners and Businesses to discover events, create relationships, and manage their agreements to broaden their selling capabilities inside Feverzone. The Concession Model is the name used for the connection between both. It is also used for the relationships between partners and Affiliates, Ambassadors, and any entity interested in selling the partner\u0027s inventory.\n\nThis will allow that the business as an entity lives publicly to all partners that are interested to work with them.\n\nThis model will enable Businesses to publish themselves as \"open\" for concessions, allowing seamless plan sales from any partner. The aim is to simplify and speed up the connection between partners and companies to increase their networks.\n\nA concession is the authorisation of two parties for one to sell the plans of the not available\nWhy\nPartners can offer discounted tickets to companies and establish better relationships (e.g., Apple employees enjoying better prices on Candlelights). This increases sales reach and the partner\u0027s branding thanks to the sales networks and promotion.\nBased on the B2C sites the system allows the Partner to create a quick website based on the original Event with specific tickets and prices.\nThe promotional page can be restricted and is not indexed so it’s only accessible to the persons who know the link or the link and the code. \nThis allows the partner to track specifically the promotion separated from normal sales.\nUse Cases\nA partner needs a private link to sell their plan at different prices for a group or collective.\nA partner wants to have a temporal promotion with special prices.\nA partner arranges a deal with another company to sell their inventory for a special price. Like employee benefits.\nA partner wants to partner with a brand to sell certain products. \nHow\nUser Manual\nCreate a Promotional Page\nSet a code for the Promotional Page\nSet specific tickets\nSet specific prices\nDelete a promotional page\nLimitations\nHow to Date().getTime();</script><script nonce="IG_qJwEgiFywPVudcarBoQ">DOCS_modelChunk = {"chunk":[{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.qyite6v6lcc"],"nmc":{"ty":"is","ibi":1,"s":"(WIP) OTAs\nOTA management is currently led by Juan Martinez Olondo. His team is responsible for maintaining and developing feber\u0027s transactional API so that it can connect with these OTAs via API. Tickets are sold entirely through the OTA channel using our API, which records sales against our inventory so that we can commission them.\n\nRight now, the Resellers team is working on adding the features that are currently being done manually to integrate them into FZ.\n\nAt the moment, the process of integrating new OTAs is manual, but in the future, our goal is to make it possible to activate and manage OTAs easily from FZ.\nUse Cases\nHow\nUser Manual\nOpt out of third-party publications\nEvents are by default suitable for publication in OTA and third-party channels. However, partners can opt out of this if they want. \u000bTo do so, there is a new section in the sidebar: Event -\u003e Event Settings.\nBy disabling the option, an email is sent to otas@feverup.com notifying about the new status, so the OTAs team can manage the situation manually (for now)."}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.qyite6v6lcc"],"nmc":{"ty":"as","st":"document","si":0,"ei":0,"sm":{"ds_lhs":1,"ds_df":{"df_dm":1},"ds_ulhfl":false}}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.qyite6v6lcc"],"nmc":{"ty":"as","st":"headings","si":0,"ei":0,"sm":{"hs_h3":{"sdef_ps":{"ps_sb_i":false,"ps_sb":16.0},"sdef_ts":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#434343","clr_type":0},"ts_bd":false}},"hs_t":{"sdef_ps":{"ps_sb_i":false,"ps_sa_i":false,"ps_sa":3.0,"ps_sb":0.0},"sdef_ts":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_fs":26.0,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fs_i":false}},"hs_h2":{"sdef_ps":{"ps_sa_i":false,"ps_sa":6.0},"sdef_ts":{"ts_bd_i":false,"ts_fs":16.0,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fs_i":false}},"hs_h1":{"sdef_ps":{"ps_sb_i":false,"ps_sb":20.0},"sdef_ts":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_fs":20.0,"ts_bd":false,"ts_fs_i":false}},"hs_nt":{"sdef_ps":{"ps_lslm":1,"ps_lslm_i":false,"ps_sm":0,"ps_sm_i":false}},"hs_st":{"sdef_ps":{"ps_sb_i":false,"ps_sa_i":false,"ps_sa":16.0,"ps_sb":0.0},"sdef_ts":{"ts_ff_i":false,"ts_it":false,"ts_fs":15.0,"ts_ff":"Arial","ts_it_i":false,"ts_fs_i":false}},"hs_h6":{"sdef_ps":{"ps_lslm":1,"ps_sb_i":true,"ps_sa_i":true,"ps_lslm_i":true,"ps_pbb_i":true,"ps_sa":0.0,"ps_sb":0.0},"sdef_ts":{"ts_it":true,"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_fs":11.0,"ts_it_i":false,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#b7b7b7","clr_type":0},"ts_bd":false,"ts_fs_i":true}},"hs_h5":{"sdef_ps":{"ps_sb_i":false,"ps_sa_i":false,"ps_sa":4.0,"ps_sb":12.0},"sdef_ts":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#666666","clr_type":0},"ts_bd":false}},"hs_h4":{"sdef_ps":{"ps_sb_i":false,"ps_sa_i":false,"ps_sa":4.0,"ps_sb":14.0},"sdef_ts":{"ts_bd_i":true,"ts_fgc2_i":false,"ts_fgc2":{"hclr_color":"#666666","clr_type":0},"ts_bd":false}}}}},{"ty":"nm","nmr":["ksm","t.qyite6v6lcc"],"nmc":{"ty":"as","st":"paragraph","si":11,"ei":11,"sm":{"ps_klt_i":true,"ps_awao_i":true,"ps_sm_i":true,"ps_ls_i":true,"ps_il_i":true,"ps_ir_i":true,"ps_al_i":true,"ps_bl_i":true,"ps_sd_i":true,"ps_hdid":"h.g4prnoa6d8x","ps_sb_i":true,"ps_sa_i
